meta.title: Hitl SDK — Human-in-the-loop for durable workflows
meta.description: A unified TypeScript SDK for human-in-the-loop approval in durable workflows. One await, suspend for hours or days, resume when a human approves.

hero.title: Make any workflow human-aware
hero.subtitle: Insert typed review steps into agents and automations — one await, suspend for hours or days, resume when a human approves in Slack, Teams, Discord, or a web inbox.
hero.notFramework: Not an agent framework — bring your own AI SDK or Workflow DevKit. Hitl SDK does one thing: the human part.

compare.title: Reliability-as-code
compare.subtitle: Move from hand-rolled queues and custom retries to durable, resumable human approval with a single await.

setup.title: Effortless setup
setup.subtitle: A simple declarative API to define and use human approval in your workflows.
setup.step1Desc: Add the core SDK and the Workflow DevKit binding.
setup.step2Desc: Use waitForHuman with typed actions inside a durable workflow.
setup.step3Desc: Expose the internal API and wire up your state backend.

channels.title: Deliver approvals anywhere
channels.subtitle: One adapter covers every Chat SDK platform. Or use the built-in web inbox.
channels.slackDesc: Block Kit cards with Approve/Deny and editable modals.
channels.discordDesc: Embeds and modals via Chat SDK.
channels.inboxDesc: Built into hitl — resolve via hitl.inbox or your own UI.

stack.title: Bring your own stack
stack.subtitle: Hitl SDK plugs into the tools you already use. It does not replace your agent framework or workflow engine.
stack.aiSdkDesc: Use familiar AI SDK patterns with durable human review steps.
stack.wdkDesc: Suspend, sleep, and resume with zero infrastructure setup on Vercel.

architecture.title: How it works
architecture.subtitle: The workflow and the server are separate processes. The .well-known/hitldev/v1 API is the only thing between them.
architecture.point1: Workflow suspends on a durable hook and POSTs a request via a memoized step.
architecture.point2: Server records the request and delivers to your channel adapter.
architecture.point3: Reviewer approves — server resolves the hook and the workflow resumes.

cta.title: Create your first approval flow today.
cta.subtitle: See Hitl SDK in action with the hello-world example.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.intro: Hitl SDK is a TypeScript library for human-in-the-loop approval in durable workflows. It is not an agent framework — bring your own AI SDK or Workflow DevKit.
gettingStarted.runDesc: The example demonstrates a Workflow DevKit workflow that suspends on waitForHuman, you submit through the web inbox, and the workflow resumes.
gettingStarted.try1: Enter a name and click Run helloWorkflow.
gettingStarted.try2: Approve the pending request in the list.
gettingStarted.try3: Check the dev server terminal for Hello, …!

installation.title: Installation
installation.intro: Install the core SDK and the engine binding for your durable execution platform.
installation.coreDesc: hitl provides the server, inbox API, and field builders. @hitl/resolver-workflow-sdk binds to Workflow DevKit.
installation.adaptersDesc: Install @hitl/adapter-chat-sdk and the Chat SDK platforms you need. One adapter covers Slack, Teams, Discord, and every Chat SDK platform.
installation.stateDesc: In-memory by default. Use @hitl/state-sqlite for local dev or @hitl/state-pg for production Postgres.

channels.title: Channels
channels.intro: Deliver approvals to reviewers on the platforms they already use.
channels.chatDesc: The @hitl/adapter-chat-sdk adapter renders native cards and routes interactivity to hitl.inbox across every Chat SDK platform.
channels.inboxDesc: Built into hitl. List, approve, and deny via hitl.inbox or expose your own endpoint — the hello-world example shows both.

workflowDevkit.title: Workflow DevKit
workflowDevkit.intro: Workflow DevKit runs workflows in a separate sandbox from your app server. The workflow client carries no state backend — it suspends and POSTs to the server over a durable step.
workflowDevkit.clientDesc: workflowHitl wraps suspend, sleep, and request primitives. Define waitForHuman in your app and call it from use workflow functions.
workflowDevkit.serverDesc: new Hitl({ state, resolver: workflowResolver() }) owns persistence and channel delivery. Export hitl.routeHandlers for the internal API.
workflowDevkit.flow1: Workflow calls waitForHuman — suspends and creates a request.
workflowDevkit.flow2: Server delivers to adapters and waits for a decision.
workflowDevkit.flow3: Reviewer approves — resolver resumes the workflow with the result.
